_schema: Map each origin's weather-fetcher region name (must match ORIGINS in backend/scripts/fetch_origin_weather.py) to NOAA STAR's internal integer provinceID for the get_TS_admin.php endpoint. Use the country's ISO-3 code at the origin level. Set province_id to null where the ID has not been confirmed yet — the fetcher logs a warning and skips that region rather than guessing.
_note: NOAA STAR operates VHI at the admin-1 level (Brazilian state, Colombian departamento, etc.). Sub-state regions our weather fetcher tracks (e.g. Sul de Minas + Cerrado both in Minas Gerais) legitimately share the same provinceID.

Montecillos._note: Montecillos coffee region spans Comayagua/La Paz/Intibucá/Santa Bárbara — Comayagua is the dominant production share.

Gayo._note: Gayo highlands sit within Aceh province.

Java._note: Java coffee is concentrated on the Ijen plateau in East Java; West/Central Java contribute little.

Flores._note: Flores is in East Nusa Tenggara (NTT). NOAA ID not yet captured.

Rwenzori._note: Rwenzori mountains sit within Kasese district (GADM admin-2).

Mt Elgon._note: Mt Elgon coffee zone (eastern slope) maps to Mbale district at GADM admin-2.

Sidama/Yirgacheffe._note: Sidama + Yirgacheffe historically inside SNNPR; GADM may still use the legacy boundary after the 2023 partition.

Harrar._note: Harrar coffee comes from East Hararghe in Oromia.
